description_long: This potted plant features a dense cluster of long, thin, pinkish-purple leaves or petals that radiate upward and outward from the center, resembling the bloom of a spider chrysanthemum. The foliage below is broad, flat, and green, forming a layered skirt around the base of the plant. The plant is anchored by a mass of exposed, twisted brown roots that spill out from the bottom, giving it a wild, organic appearance. The pot itself is not distinctly visible, as the roots and foliage obscure its form, but the plant appears to be self-contained and upright. The overall look is striking and ornamental, with a dramatic contrast between the vibrant upper leaves and the earthy, tangled roots. This type of plant would serve as a decorative accent in urban environments, adding a touch of nature to otherwise structured spaces.
description: A striking potted plant with pink, spiky blooms, broad green leaves, and exposed, twisted brown roots. The plant has a wild, ornamental appearance ideal for decorative urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full spread of pink leaves, dense green foliage, and a prominent tangle of brown roots at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the layered green leaves and the way the roots cascade downward, partially obscuring the pot.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the symmetrical arrangement of the green leaves and the central cluster of pink leaves, with roots visible below.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the arching pink leaves and the wild, sprawling nature of the roots.
attribute_pot_type: obscured (likely plastic or ceramic, but not visible)
